content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of all the issues. Let me create a detailed investigation report. # COMPREHENSIVE UI/UX INVESTIGATION REPORT ## Executive Summary I've conducted a thorough investigation of the 7 reported issues plus proactive identification of related problems. This report provides root cause analysis, file locations, code snippets, and recommended fix approaches for each issue. --- ## ISSUE 1: ESC Key Still Closes AI Terminal Despite useEscapeKey Hook ❌ CRITICAL ### Root Cause Analysis **PRIORITY CONFLICT LOGIC ERROR** - The useEscapeKey hook has a **REVERSED priority system** that causes lower priority handlers to execute FIRST instead of LAST. ### File Location & Code **File:** `src/hooks/useEscapeKey.ts` (Lines 26-41) **Current Implementation:** ```typescript const handleEscape = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { if (e.key === 'Escape') { // Check if a higher priority handler already processed this event const currentPriority = (e as any)._escPriority || -1; if (priority > currentPriority) { // ❌ BUG HERE // Mark this priority on the event (e as any)._escPriority = priority; e.stopPropagation(); e.preventDefault(); onEscape(); } } }; ``` **Priority Values:** - Modal (backup prompt): `priority: 9999` (Line 23 in Modal.tsx) - AI Terminal: `priority: 40` (Line 115 in AITerminal.tsx) ### The Problem 1. User opens backup prompt → Modal sets `_escPriority = 9999` 2. User presses ESC 3. **AI Terminal handler runs first** (due to capture phase event listener order) 4. Check: `40 > 9999` → **FALSE** → AI Terminal handler SHOULD skip 5. Modal handler runs second 6. Check: `9999 > 9999` → **FALSE** → Modal ALSO skips! **Result:** Neither handler executes because the logic is backwards! When the Modal runs FIRST (random order): 1. Modal sets `_escPriority = 9999` and closes 2. AI Terminal checks: `40 > 9999` → FALSE → AI Terminal closes too! ### Recommended Fix **Reverse the comparison logic:** ```typescript if (priority >= currentPriority) { // Higher or equal priority wins (e as any)._escPriority = priority; e.stopPropagation(); e.preventDefault(); onEscape(); } ``` **Or simpler: use event listener registration order:** Since capture phase listeners execute in REGISTRATION order (first registered = first executed), simply ensure modals register BEFORE terminal by using a useEffect dependency array or higher useLayoutEffect. --- ## ISSUE 2: AI Terminal Button Covering Dashboard Buttons ✅ LAYOUT CONFLICT ### Root Cause Analysis **Z-INDEX STACKING CONFLICT** - AI Terminal button (`z-40`) overlaps Dashboard header buttons which likely have lower or no z-index. ### File Locations **AI Terminal Open Button Location:** Looking at Header.tsx or similar component - the AI Terminal toggle button is positioned `top-0 right-0` with `z-40`. **Dashboard Button Location:** **File:** `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` (Lines 256-271) ```typescript
``` ### The Problem The buttons are positioned at `top-right` of the page header (Lines 256-271), directly where the AI Terminal button is positioned (`fixed top-0 right-0 z-40`). ### Recommended Fix **Move Dashboard buttons below the heading** as user requested: **Current Structure (Lines 251-276):** ```typescript

Dashboard

{/* ❌ Buttons here */}

Your personal management overview

``` **Proposed Structure:** ```typescript

Dashboard

Your personal management overview

{/* ✅ Buttons moved here */}
``` This places buttons directly beneath the heading text, in the open horizontal space next to the description. --- ## ISSUE 3: Notes Editor Small Square Box PERSISTS Despite w-full Fix ❌ CRITICAL ### Root Cause Analysis **PARENT CONTAINER WIDTH CONSTRAINT** - While ContentEditable has `w-full`, its PARENT container has restrictive sizing. ### File Location & Code **File:** `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` (Lines 724-748) **Current Structure:** ```typescript
{/* ✅ Full width */}
{/* ❌ max-w-4xl! */}
{/* ❌ Constrained by parent */} {/* ❌ w-full of PARENT, not viewport */} } ... />
``` ### The Problem **Visual Diagram:** ``` Viewport (100%) └─ overflow-y-auto (100%) └─ max-w-4xl mx-auto (896px centered!) ❌ BOTTLENECK └─ border container (896px - 64px padding = 832px) └─ ContentEditable w-full (832px) ← User sees "small box" ``` The `max-w-4xl` class limits the editor to **896px** and centers it with `mx-auto`, creating large white margins on wider screens. ### Recommended Fix **Remove the max-w-4xl constraint:** ```typescript
{/* ✅ Changed max-w-4xl to w-full */}
``` **Or make it responsive:** ```typescript
{/* Full width always */} ``` --- ## ISSUE 4: Planning Page Event Creation - Dark Mode White Text on White Background ❌ CRITICAL ### Root Cause Analysis **INCONSISTENT COLOR TOKENS** - Event creation modal uses OLD color classes (`neutral-lightGray`, `neutral-darkGray`, `neutral-charcoal`) instead of semantic tokens. ### File Locations & Code **File:** `src/widgets/Calendar.tsx` (Multiple locations) **Line 572** - Description textarea: ```typescript
